<!-- type: tutorial -->

# [Your Tutorial Title Here]

> **When to use this template:** You're writing a guided learning experience for someone getting started with something new or re-orienting after a gap. Tutorials walk readers through a hands-on experience step-by-step with a warm, encouraging tone. Good tutorials answer "How do I get started?" and "What should I learn first?"

## Overview

<!-- 
What will the reader learn by following this tutorial?
Who should read this? (experience level, prerequisites)
How long will it take?
What will they have accomplished by the end?

1-2 paragraphs. Warm, encouraging tone.
Example:
"In this tutorial, you'll set up your local development environment 
from scratch. By the end, you'll have a working project and understand 
how all the pieces fit together. This takes about 30 minutes."
-->

## Before You Start

<!-- 
What does the reader need to have or know before starting?
- Software installed (Node.js, Python, etc.)
- Prior knowledge (Git basics, command line)
- Access to resources (API keys, GitHub repo access)
- Time/hardware requirements

Use a bulleted list or numbered checklist.
-->

## Step 1: [First Action]

<!-- 
Numbered step with imperative mood ("Run X", "Open Y", "Create Z").
Include the actual command or action.
Add context if needed, but keep it focused.
Include expected output so reader knows they're on track.

Example:
"Clone the repository:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/user/repo.git
cd repo
```
You should see a directory listing of the repo contents."
-->

What mistakes do people commonly make?
Why do they happen?
How do you fix them?

Run: source venv/bin/activate"
